inline_comments.message:
Could you please explain why the port and the targetPort are different? Per k8s documentation (https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/), "By default the targetPort will be set to the same value as the port field."
Is this differentiation really necessary?

The code shows that 
* CLI pod exposes port 80 and 8080.
* the CLI service is mapping port 80 to 8080, and port 8080 to 9090.

Would be good to have a bit explanation about this in the description. ~.~
port 8080 is used for? should it show up in readinessProbe too?

Because https://git.onap.org/cli/tree/deployment/docker/src/main/docker/docker-compose.yaml describes the necessary port mapping: services: occ: image: onap/cli environment: OPEN_CLI_MODE: 'daemon' ports: - 8080:80 - 9090:8080 If you ssh in CLI container, you can find there are 2 exposed port, 80 for lighttpd, 8080 for gotty to provide CLI on webpage. If you login in vnc-portal, you click the CLI icon, it tries to access port 8080, so 8080 port should be mapped to container port 80. You can also check the https://git.onap.org/cli/tree/deployment/http/web/index.html. If you click link to open CLI in webpage, it will access port 9090, so port 9090 should be mapped to container 8080. I have implemented this solution, currently we can use 8080 to access web page and 9090 to access CLI. onap@onap-ProLiant-DL360-Gen9:~/setup/oom-amsterdam/kubernetes/cli/templates$ kubectl exec -it cli-1971128182-p1mwg bash -n onap-cli root@cli-1971128182-p1mwg:/opt/onap/cli# netstat -nltp Active Internet connections (only servers) Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address Foreign Address State PID/Program name tcp 0 0 0.0.0.0:80 0.0.0.0:* LISTEN - tcp6 0 0 :::8080 :::* LISTEN 16/gotty https://git.onap.org/cli/tree/deployment/http/web/index.html: function redirectToConsole(){ window.location.href="http://"+ window.location.hostname + ":9090"; }

Click here

Because https://git.onap.org/cli/tree/deployment/docker/src/main/docker/docker-compose.yaml describes the necessary port mapping: services: occ: image: onap/cli environment: OPEN_CLI_MODE: 'daemon' ports: - 8080:80 - 9090:8080 If you ssh in CLI container, you can find there are 2 exposed port, 80 for lighttpd, 8080 for gotty to provide CLI on webpage. If you login in vnc-portal, you click the CLI icon, it tries to access port 8080, so 8080 port should be mapped to container port 80. You can also check the https://git.onap.org/cli/tree/deployment/http/web/index.html. If you click link to open CLI in webpage, it will access port 9090, so port 9090 should be mapped to container 8080. I have implemented this solution, currently we can use 8080 to access web page and 9090 to access CLI. onap@onap-ProLiant-DL360-Gen9:~/setup/oom-amsterdam/kubernetes/cli/templates$ kubectl exec -it cli-1971128182-p1mwg bash -n onap-cli root@cli-1971128182-p1mwg:/opt/onap/cli# netstat -nltp Active Internet connections (only servers) Proto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address Foreign Address State PID/Program name tcp 0 0 0.0.0.0:80 0.0.0.0:* LISTEN - tcp6 0 0 :::8080 :::* LISTEN 16/gotty https://git.onap.org/cli/tree/deployment/http/web/index.html: function redirectToConsole(){ window.location.href="http://"+ window.location.hostname + ":9090"; }

Click here

80 for lighttpd providing web service.
8080 for gotty providing an TTY(CLI) on web page.

I am not sure if it need to be added to readinessProbe. But when we try to access CLI, first we use 80 port to access web page, then we click a link on this web page to access 8080 in order to open CLI.

Do you have any suggestion about it? What's the necessity to add a readinessProbe?
